notes: EGAT government course at Rajjaprabha Dam (Ratchaprapha Dam), Ban Ta Khun District, Surat Thani. Near Khao Sok National Park and Cheow Lan Lake. 18H par 72, opened 2000, EGAT design. GPS confirmed 8.9582/98.8029. Dual pricing: Thai nationals ~500 THB weekday; foreigners ~1,000 THB (confirmed across aggregators and TripAdvisor reviews 2019-2023). Caddie 300 THB, cart 600 THB — available but mandatory status unconfirmed. Club rental 700 THB. Phone +66 77 242562 (most cited; 1golf.eu shows different number). No official website. ~40 km from Surat Thani city. ~670 km from Bangkok. Hilly terrain — cart strongly recommended. Described as 'hidden gem' consistently. Last confirmed operating 2023.

green_fee_weekday_thb.note: PROVISIONAL — foreigner rate confirmed across multiple aggregators and TripAdvisor reviews. Thai nationals pay ~500 THB weekday per 1golf.eu. Dual pricing is confirmed practice at this course.

green_fee_weekend_thb.note: PROVISIONAL — 1golf.eu shows 1,000 THB for weekend. Foreigner weekend rate likely same or higher.

cart_fee_thb.note: PROVISIONAL — golfasian.com. Strongly recommended given hilly terrain.

phone.note: Conflict: 1golf.eu shows +66 77 240 7405. Using most-cited number as primary.

prose.overview: Rajjaprabha Dam Golf Course is one of Thailand's best-kept secrets — an 18-hole, par 72 EGAT government course built at the Rajjaprabha Dam (Ratchaprapha Dam) in Ban Ta Khun District, Surat Thani, on the edge of Khao Sok National Park. Opened in 2000 and operated by the Electricity Generating Authority of Thailand, it follows the same institutional pattern as Ubolratana Dam in Khon Kaen and Bhumibol Dam in Tak: a government agency course built for its workforce and the surrounding community, accessible to civilian golfers at unusually low prices. For foreign visitors, the green fee runs approximately 1,000 THB — one of the most affordable 18-hole rounds in southern Thailand — with caddie (300 THB) and cart (600 THB) available separately. The setting beside the Cheow Lan reservoir in the Khao Sok highlands makes this among the most visually spectacular government courses in the country, with a layout that works the hilly terrain overlooking the dam and its surrounding jungle.
prose.layout_and_experience:
Rajjaprabha Dam Golf Course sits in the highland interior of Surat Thani province, roughly 40 km from the provincial capital in the forested hills that form the watershed above the Cheow Lan reservoir. The terrain is genuinely hilly — the course is routinely described as requiring a cart given the elevation changes between holes, though cart hire is technically optional rather than mandated.

At 6,820 yards from the blue tees, the layout is full-length for a par 72, stretching across terrain shaped by the dam's catchment geography. Holes work the natural contours of the hillside rather than flattening them into a resort-style layout: tee shots often play from elevated platforms with fairways dropping or rising in the distance, and approach shots require adjustment for the slope of greens that follow the natural grade. The result is a course that rewards local knowledge — which is why a caddie at 300 THB is a worthwhile investment for first-time visitors.

Because the course serves both a Thai workforce clientele and a growing stream of Khao Sok adventure tourists, the atmosphere is relaxed and genuinely local — a contrast to the more polished resort golf of Koh Samui and Phuket. Maintenance is appropriate for a government facility: well-maintained and playable, without the manicured precision of a five-star resort product.

The course's dual pricing (Thai nationals pay approximately 500 THB on weekdays; foreigners approximately 1,000 THB) is a standard practice at government-operated venues and does not affect the quality of the round. The all-in total for a foreign visitor — green fee plus caddie plus cart — is approximately 1,900 THB, making this one of the best-value full rounds in the south.
prose.tips:
Rajjaprabha Dam Golf Course is best reached by car — the course is approximately 40 km from Surat Thani city centre (about one hour's drive) and there is no public transport to the dam area. From Khao Lak or Phuket, the drive is approximately two hours; from Koh Samui via ferry, allow three hours total. Rental cars from Surat Thani city or the airport are the most practical option.

Call +66 77 242562 ahead to confirm current green fees and tee time availability — the course has no website and listing-site prices may lag real rates by a year or more. Dual pricing (Thai nationals vs. foreigners) is confirmed; the foreigner rate is approximately 1,000 THB green fee. Cart hire (600 THB) is strongly recommended given the hilly terrain. Club rental is available at 700 THB per set.

The course is adjacent to the Rajjaprabha Dam visitor area and the ferry departure point for Cheow Lan Lake (Khao Sok). Pairing a round with a Cheow Lan Lake boat trip or an overnight at one of the floating bungalows is a natural itinerary: golf in the morning, lake in the afternoon. The Khao Sok area receives heavy rainfall year-round (it is one of Thailand's wettest regions), so the April–May and October–November shoulder seasons may bring interruptions. January to March is typically the driest window.
prose.location_and_access: Rajjaprabha Dam Golf Course is located at 53 Moo 3, Ratchaprapha Dam, Khao Phang, Ban Ta Khun District, Surat Thani 84230 — approximately 40 km from Surat Thani city and 670 km south of Bangkok. Surat Thani Airport is served by multiple daily flights from Bangkok (approximately 1.5 hours from Don Mueang or Suvarnabhumi). From the airport, the dam is one hour by rental car or taxi. For visitors from Koh Samui, the ferry to Surat Thani pier (Don Sak) takes 1.5 hours, with an additional 1.5-hour drive to the dam. From Khao Lak: approximately two hours via Highway 401 and Route 4009. Phone: +66 77 242562.
prose.rental_cta_context: Combining Khao Sok and a round at Rajjaprabha Dam? LENGOLF can kit you out with premium rental clubs in Bangkok before you head south — travel light through Surat Thani without hauling a bag through the jungle.
